channel.title: Frontion News Headlines
channel.description: Breaking geopolitical news from Tier 1 sources

item.title: US and Iran Near Short-Term Peace Deal
item.description: The United States and Iran are edging toward a temporary agreement to halt their war, with Tehran reviewing a proposal that would stop the fighting but leave contentious issues unresolved.

item.title: Ukraine Launches Major Drone Attack on Moscow
item.description: Russia reported shooting down 347 Ukrainian drones overnight in a major attack after Moscow shunned a ceasefire offer, marking one of the largest drone assaults on the Russian capital.

item.title: Dollar Faces Trapdoor if Iran War Ends
item.description: Fresh optimism over a possible end to the Iran war has pushed the US dollar back to pre-conflict lows, with analysts warning the greenback could be a casualty of any peace deal.

item.title: Japan Signals Unlimited Yen Defense
item.description: Japan faces no constraints on currency market interventions and is in daily contact with US authorities, reinforcing Tokyo resolve to defend the embattled yen amid global volatility.

item.title: What At Stake at Trump-Xi Summit
item.description: President Trump is scheduled to meet Chinese President Xi Jinping in Beijing next week, as both countries seek to stabilize a relationship strained by tensions over trade, Taiwan and the Iran war.

item.title: US Sanctions Cuba Nickel Operation Targeting China-Linked Supply Chains
item.description: Marco Rubio announced new sanctions on Thursday, escalating the Trump administration pressure campaign against Havana. The move targets nickel operations with implications for China-linked critical mineral supply chains.

item.title: US Intelligence Estimates Iran Can Outlast Hormuz Blockade for Months
item.description: Iran retains about 75% of prewar inventories of mobile launchers and 70% of missile stockpiles, according to a Washington Post report. The assessment suggests Tehran has significant resilience despite ongoing conflict.

item.title: Paris Prosecutor Opens Judicial Investigation Into Elon Musk and X
item.description: Paris public prosecutor has opened a formal judicial investigation into Elon Musk's X social media platform, marking a new step in an ongoing probe.

item.title: US Iran Resolution at UN Faces Likely Vetoes from China and Russia
item.description: The US urged countries to support its UN resolution demanding Iran halt attacks and mining of waterways. China and Russia are expected to veto the measure.

item.title: UK Press Watchdog Rules Publications Can Call Gaza War a Genocide
item.description: The Independent Press Standards Organisation has ruled that publications are entitled to describe Israel's actions in Gaza as genocide, a significant media ethics decision.

item.title: French prosecutors seek charges against Elon Musk and X over abuse images and Grok deepfakes
item.description: Paris prosecutors opened an investigation into X for complicity in distributing child sexual abuse images, deepfakes, and Holocaust denial by the AI system Grok. Musk and former X CEO Yaccarino were summoned for voluntary interviews but did not appear.

item.title: Trump FEMA council proposes sweeping changes to US disaster aid system
item.description: A council appointed by President Trump approved recommendations to overhaul FEMA, potentially reducing federal disaster support and shifting more responsibility to states. The changes would affect how disasters qualify for federal aid and how states receive reimbursement.

item.title: EU Leaders Prepare for Potential Talks With Putin
item.description: European Union leaders are preparing for potential talks with Russian President Vladimir Putin, according to a senior official cited by the Financial Times. The move signals a possible shift in diplomatic strategy as the Ukraine conflict continues.

item.title: Saudi Arabia and Kuwait Lift US Military Access Restrictions
item.description: Saudi Arabia and Kuwait have lifted restrictions on US military use of their bases and airspace that were imposed after the launch of Operation to reopen the Strait of Hormuz, the Wall Street Journal reports.

item.title: UK Elections Could Reshape British Politics
item.description: Elections for nearly 5,000 council seats in England and parliaments in Scotland and Wales could signal the end of Britain's traditional two-party system if voters opt for populist and nationalist parties.

item.title: US and China Weigh Launch of Official AI Discussions
item.description: Washington and Beijing are weighing the launch of official discussions about artificial intelligence, the Wall Street Journal reports, as competition in the critical technology sector intensifies.

item.title: Canada Dollar Outlook Bullish on Geopolitical Risk Easing
item.description: The Canadian dollar is expected to strengthen if uncertainty linked to the Middle East war and US tariffs eases, according to a Reuters poll, as markets eye potential resolution of the Iran conflict.

item.title: US-Germany Rift Deepens Over Iran War as Trump Orders Troop Withdrawal
item.description: German Chancellor Merz criticized Trump for having "no strategy" on Iran, prompting the US to announce withdrawal of 5,000 troops from Germany. Berlin dispatched minesweepers to the Mediterranean as the diplomatic row escalates.

item.title: Satellite Images Show Iran Hit 228 US Military Targets, Far More Than Reported
item.description: Analysis of satellite photos reveals damage to at least 228 structures at US bases across the Persian Gulf region. A new report estimates US spent $72 billion on the Iran war—nearly triple the Pentagon official estimate.

item.title: Iran Reviews US Peace Proposal Conveyed Through Pakistan, No Final Decision Yet
item.description: Iran confirmed it is reviewing a US ceasefire proposal transmitted via Pakistani mediation, described as a "14-point framework." Tehran has not yet responded to Washington as markets rally on hopes of Hormuz reopening.

item.title: China Sentences Former Defense Ministers to Suspended Death
item.description: China has announced suspended death sentences for former defense ministers Wei Fenghe and Li Shangfu following their ousting in a major military purge. The move highlights ongoing anti-corruption campaigns targeting top military brass.

item.title: Three IS-Linked Women Arrested in Australia After Syria Return
item.description: Three women allegedly linked to Islamic State have been arrested in Australia after flying home from Syria for the first time in years. The arrests highlight ongoing concerns about returning foreign fighters.

item.title: Hungary Returns Seized $82 Million in Cash and Gold to Ukraine
item.description: Hungary has returned a seized shipment worth $82 million in cash and gold to Ukraine, in a significant gesture after initially confiscating the assets. The move could signal improving Hungary-Ukraine relations.

item.title: Ukraine Gripen Fighter Deal Could Be Signed Within Months
item.description: Ukraine's purchase of Swedish Saab Gripen fighter jets could be finalized within months, Sweden's defense minister announced during talks in Stockholm, marking a significant boost to Ukraine's air defense capabilities.

item.title: Israel Strikes Beirut, Netanyahu Warns No Immunity for Militants
item.description: Israel carried out its first strike on Beirut's southern suburbs since the ceasefire, targeting a Hezbollah commander. Netanyahu declared there would be "no immunity" for Israel's enemies amid escalating regional tensions.
